title: Pleopeltis cochlearis
intro: Pleopeltis cochlearis, commonly known as the cochlearis polypody, is a species of epiphytic fern native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems. The bioactive constituents of Pleopeltis cochlearis include glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote immune function and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
taxonomy: Pleopeltis cochlearis is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and phylogenetic relationships. The family Polypodiaceae is a group of vascular plants commonly known as the polypody ferns. The genus Pleopeltis is a part of this family and is native to the Americas. Pleopeltis cochlearis is one of the many species within this genus.
